agent_guidelines.customization_note: This document structure is flexible. Sections and subsections can be added or removed as needed for different projects. Ensure that agent_guidelines and section-specific agent_instructions are updated to reflect how new nodes should be used or filled.
agent_guidelines.document_structure: Tabs > Sections > Subsections > blocks[]. Section titles ARE the dict keys — use them verbatim. Never regenerate the full document; find the exact key path and update only that node.

key_locations.next_topic: tabs[id=research] > sections['Open Research Queue'] > blocks[type=table] — highest-priority OPEN row
key_locations.rejection_index: tabs[id=research] > sections['Discarded Alternatives'] — ALL verdict.label fields = permanent closed list
key_locations.tracker: tabs[id=research] > sections['Research Tracker'] — update Status after each session
key_locations.theory_specs: tabs[id=theory] > sections['Theoretical Foundations'] — one locked spec per subsection
key_locations.session_notes: tabs[id=research] > sections[TOPIC_KEY] — companion to each theory spec: Sources table, Decisions, Rejected claims
key_locations.changelog: tabs[id=changelog] > sections keyed by version — newest first, current=true on latest

research_session_protocol.name: Rejection preflight
research_session_protocol.action: Read ALL verdict blocks in Discarded Alternatives. If the topic appears → STOP, approach is closed.

research_session_protocol.name: Tracker preflight
research_session_protocol.action: Read Research Tracker rows for the topic. Note current Status and open questions.

research_session_protocol.name: Theory inventory
research_session_protocol.action: Check Theoretical Foundations subsections. If spec exists, read it fully before adding.

research_session_protocol.name: Research — source rules
research_session_protocol.action: Tier 1 only as primary evidence: arXiv, NeurIPS, ICML, JMLR, JCF, JFEC, RFS. Tier 2: official docs. No blogs, Medium, Reddit, forums.

research_session_protocol.name: Second-opinion evaluation
research_session_protocol.action: Check: (a) Has authors + venue + DOI/arXiv ID — missing = TIER:NEVER. (b) 'Obras citadas'/'Referencias' in English bibliography = AI fingerprint, reject immediately. (c) Spot-check 3+ citations for existence.

research_session_protocol.name: Lock decisions
research_session_protocol.action: For each open question: one-sentence decision + Tier-1 citation + rejected alternatives (these go to Discarded Alternatives).

research_session_protocol.name: Update document
research_session_protocol.action: Surgical updates only. Insert new changelog key at TOP.

source_tiers.tier_1: arXiv, NeurIPS, ICML, JMLR, JCF, JFEC, RFS, AAAI, ICLR
source_tiers.tier_2: Official library documentation, academic textbooks (e.g. Shreve, Gatheral)
source_tiers.never: Blogs, Medium, Reddit, forums, PDFs without authors/DOI/venue, AI-generated documents
ai_fingerprints: No authors, no venue, no DOI or arXiv ID
Citations that cannot be verified or contain hallucinated papers
Domain-contaminated refs (e.g. medical journals cited for trading)

failure_modes: Adding References rows without descending version order.
Renaming existing section keys — this breaks all cross-links.
Proposing approaches that appear in Discarded Alternatives.
Using Tier 3+ sources as primary evidence.
Setting current=true on a new changelog entry without setting previous current=false.
Updating large sections instead of the specific subsection that changed.

meta.title: Research Buddy Project
meta.subtitle: Master Class Research
meta.language: English — all document content, section text, queue entries, changelog entries, and agent output must be written in English. No exceptions.

blocks.md: Welcome to your AI-ready research project. This document is structured for high-integrity collaboration between human researchers and AI agents.

Methodology.subtitle: Standards for sourcing and validating information

blocks.md: This section defines the rules for how research is conducted.

blocks.title: Mandatory pre-flight — complete before writing any output
blocks.md:
All agents conducting research or evaluation share a documented failure pattern: generating well-formatted output without reading the document context first. The following pre-flight sequence is mandatory for every agent in every mode. Output produced without completing it is invalid.

**Before writing a single sentence of analysis:**

1. Extract every `verdict` block label from [Discarded Alternatives](#discarded-alternatives){tab=research}. Any matching concept is an immediate reject.
2. Extract every row from [Research Tracker](#research-tracker){tab=research} tables. Researched topics are closed.
3. Verify first-author attribution for every cited paper.
4. Check Theoretical Foundations sections for architectural overlap.
5. Apply source tier filters (Research Methodology).
Chronological log of major technical decisions.
Prevents re-introduction of concepts already proven invalid.
Core technical specifications and theoretical foundations.
